Based on the retrieved evidence, while some autosomal recessive disorders exist—such as Juvenile Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis (JNCL/Batten disease)—they appear relatively uncommon globally and vary significantly across ethnic groups. More importantly, numerous major monogenic disorders—including type IIa hyperlipidemia and familial hemiplegic migraines—are explicitly stated to follow autosomal dominant inheritance patterns, not recessive ones. Therefore, recessive coding variants are not responsible for the majority of undiagnosed cases in non-consanguineous individuals, supporting the original conclusion.
